attacking_style: Relies on tactical patience and sprint finish rather than long-range attacks. Prefers to convert team-controlled situations into winning moves in final kilometers, often through bonus seconds or two-up scenarios.
team_tactics: Benefits from full team tempo control on climbs, particularly Sky/Ineos train setting up selective finishes. Positioned as protected rider with freedom to capitalize on sprint speed when opportunity arises.
terrain_preference: Selective finishes on punchy Ardennes-style climbs where tactical positioning and finishing kick matter more than pure climbing watts. Struggles when racing requires sustained dominance rather than tactical conversion.
vulnerability: Declining form on pure Ardennes classics (6th at Fleche despite full support). Opportunistic attacks in chaotic situations get rejected by organized opposition. Cannot sustain leads through climbing dominance alone.
key_patterns: Converts team-controlled races into wins through superior sprint/tactical timing (Tirreno, Amstel)
Relies on bonus seconds and tactical margins rather than dominant climbing (Paris-Nice, Tirreno)
Shows tactical patience, avoiding premature commitment even when appearing hesitant (Amstel 2022)
Opportunistic attacks without team setup get rejected by organized pelotons (Tour 2021)
Form decline evident in Ardennes classics despite former success (6th Fleche 2020)
form_trajectory: negative: Despite 2022 Amstel win, pattern shows shift from Ardennes dominance to tactical opportunism, with 2020 Fleche result suggesting declining raw climbing form
